issues.comment: Origin validation uses indexOf() which allows substring matches, causing security bypass. `discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1` should be `e.origin !== discourseUrl` or proper origin comparison, as current code allows any origin containing the discourse URL as substring.
ERB syntax error with `<%- end if %>` which should be `<% end %>`. The trailing `if` keyword is invalid syntax and will cause template compilation to fail.
XSS vulnerability using `raw post.cooked` without HTML escaping. User-generated content in post.cooked could contain malicious scripts that execute in the embedded context.

JavaScript injection vulnerability by interpolating `request.referer` directly into JavaScript string. If referer contains single quotes or JavaScript code, it will break/execute in the script context.
XSS vulnerability by interpolating unescaped `url` parameter into HTML anchor tag. URL could contain malicious HTML/JavaScript that executes when the content is rendered.
SSRF vulnerability using `open()` on user-controlled `SiteSetting.feed_polling_url`. Attacker could set URL to access internal services, file:// URLs, or other restricted resources.
NoMethodError if `i.content` is nil when calling `.scrub`. Should check `i.content.present?` or use safe navigation `i.content&.scrub` before processing.
URI parsing with `URI(url)` can raise URI::InvalidURIError for malformed URLs but exception is not caught in this method, causing method to crash on invalid URLs.

Test expects `TopicRetriever.new` to be called directly, but actual controller implementation calls `Jobs.enqueue(:retrieve_topic)` instead. Test doesn't match implementation behavior and will fail or pass for wrong reasons.
